<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tashiro-Ōta Kofun]][[en:Tashiroōta Kofun]][[ja:田代太田古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tashiro-Ōta Kofun]][[en:Tashiroōta Kofun]][[ja:田代太田古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tashiro-Ōta Kofun]][[en:Tashiroōta Kofun]][[ja:田代太田古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24861136]] Wikidata name: Tashiro-Ōta Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tashiroōta Kofun | native_name =田代太田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Tashiro-Ota Kofun 2024.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tashiroōta Kofun | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tashiroōta Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33.394805555556|N|130.51516666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tosu|WD=Q860674}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|41.3|N|130|30|54.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tashiroōta Kofun'''|田代太田古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tashirohonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tosu, Saga|Tosu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1926.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202916|title=田代太田古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Because the Kofun is located on the grounds of a private house, and there are preservation issues, it is closed to the public except for a special exhibition held once a year.
==Overview== The Tashiroōta Kofun is located on a hill between the Oki River and the Yamashita River, about 51 meters above sea level. The hill extends southeast from the 312.3 meter Shashigamine, which is a tributary peak of the Sefuri Mountains. It is a round {{nihono|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a dimater of 42-meters and a height of six meters, constructed in two tiers. However, due to urban encroachment, part of the tumulus was destroyed, so much remains unknown regarding its form and scale. In the surrounding area are many {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tumuli.
A fragment of a cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found on the tumulus. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry structure, consisting of a front chamber, a middle chamber, and a rear chamber, orientated to the south. It has a length of approximately nine meters, and contains corpse beds for three bodies in the rear chamber and two bodies in the middle chamber. [[:en:X-ray fluorescence|X-ray fluorescence]] analysis has revealed that the deceased were laid down without using wooden coffins. The front chamber appears to have originally been a passageway, but was modified at some unknown time. It has a flat ceiling, whereas the middle and rear chambers have a high, dome-shaped ceiling.
The 2.3 meter wide and 1.1 meter high waist stone on the back wall of the burial chamber and the wall on the right side of the middle chamber are decorated with continuous triangular patterns, using the three colors of red, black, and green on the [[:en:Granite|Granite]] rock surface. The drawings also depict people, ships, shields, ''takatsuki'' (tall cups), [[:en:Bracken|Bracken]] (''warabi'') patterns, concentric circles, and flowers. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included beads, pieces of iron arrowheads, pieces of [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]es, shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From Tosu Station on the JR Kagoshima Main Line, get off at Nishitetsu Bus "Tashiro Koen Iriguchi" and walk 5 minutes.

The 2.3 meter wide and 1.1 meter high waist stone on the back wall of the burial chamber and the wall on the right side of the middle chamber are decorated with continuous triangular patterns, using the three colors of red, black, and green on the {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} rock surface. The drawings also depict people, ships, shields, ''takatsuki'' (tall cups), {{ill|Bracken|en|Bracken|WD=Q258034|lt=Bracken}} (''warabi'') patterns, concentric circles, and flowers. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included beads, pieces of iron arrowheads, pieces of {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}es, shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The {{nihongo|'''Tashiroōta Kofun'''|田代太田古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tashirohonmachi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tosu, Saga|en|Tosu, Saga|WD=Q860674|lt=Tosu}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. ==Overview== The Tashiroōta Kofun is located on a hill between the Oki River and the Yamashita River, about 51 meters above sea level. The hill extends southeast from the 312.3 meter Shashigamine, which is a tributary peak of the Sefuri Mountains. It is a round {{nihono|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a dimater of 42-meters and a height of six meters, constructed in two tiers. The drawings also depict people, ships, shields, ''takatsuki'' (tall cups), {{ill|Bracken|en|Bracken|WD=Q258034|lt=Bracken}} (''warabi'') patterns, concentric circles, and flowers. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included beads, pieces of iron arrowheads, pieces of {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}es, shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1926.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202916|title=田代太田古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Because the Kofun is located on the grounds of a private house, and there are preservation issues, it is closed to the public except for a special exhibition held once a year.
==Overview== The Tashiroōta Kofun is located on a hill between the Oki River and the Yamashita River, about 51 meters above sea level. The hill extends southeast from the 312.3 meter Shashigamine, which is a tributary peak of the Sefuri Mountains. It is a round {{nihono|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a dimater of 42-meters and a height of six meters, constructed in two tiers. However, due to urban encroachment, part of the tumulus was destroyed, so much remains unknown regarding its form and scale. In the surrounding area are many {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tumuli.
A fragment of a cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' has been found on the tumulus. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry structure, consisting of a front chamber, a middle chamber, and a rear chamber, orientated to the south. It has a length of approximately nine meters, and contains corpse beds for three bodies in the rear chamber and two bodies in the middle chamber. {{ill|X-ray fluorescence|en|X-ray fluorescence|WD=Q898974|lt=X-ray fluorescence}} analysis has revealed that the deceased were laid down without using wooden coffins. The front chamber appears to have originally been a passageway, but was modified at some unknown time. It has a flat ceiling, whereas the middle and rear chambers have a high, dome-shaped ceiling.
The 2.3 meter wide and 1.1 meter high waist stone on the back wall of the burial chamber and the wall on the right side of the middle chamber are decorated with continuous triangular patterns, using the three colors of red, black, and green on the {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} rock surface. The drawings also depict people, ships, shields, ''takatsuki'' (tall cups), {{ill|Bracken|en|Bracken|WD=Q258034|lt=Bracken}} (''warabi'') patterns, concentric circles, and flowers. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included beads, pieces of iron arrowheads, pieces of {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}es, shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From Tosu Station on the JR Kagoshima Main Line, get off at Nishitetsu Bus "Tashiro Koen Iriguchi" and walk 5 minutes.

From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From Tosu Station on the JR Kagoshima Main Line, get off at Nishitetsu Bus "Tashiro Koen Iriguchi" and walk 5 minutes.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tashiroōta Kofun | native_name =田代太田古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Tashiro-Ota Kofun 2024.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tashiroōta Kofun | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tashiroōta Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33.394805555556|N|130.51516666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tosu|WD=Q860674|1=Tosu, Saga}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|41.3|N|130|30|54.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tashiroōta Kofun'''|田代太田古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tashirohonmachi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tosu, Saga|en|Tosu, Saga|WD=Q860674|lt=Tosu|1=Tosu, Saga}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1926.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202916|title=田代太田古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Because the Kofun is located on the grounds of a private house, and there are preservation issues, it is closed to the public except for a special exhibition held once a year.
==Overview== The Tashiroōta Kofun is located on a hill between the Oki River and the Yamashita River, about 51 meters above sea level. The hill extends southeast from the 312.3 meter Shashigamine, which is a tributary peak of the Sefuri Mountains. It is a round {{nihono|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a dimater of 42-meters and a height of six meters, constructed in two tiers. However, due to urban encroachment, part of the tumulus was destroyed, so much remains unknown regarding its form and scale. In the surrounding area are many {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tumuli.
A fragment of a cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been found on the tumulus. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry structure, consisting of a front chamber, a middle chamber, and a rear chamber, orientated to the south. It has a length of approximately nine meters, and contains corpse beds for three bodies in the rear chamber and two bodies in the middle chamber. {{ill|X-ray fluorescence|en|X-ray fluorescence|WD=Q898974|lt=X-ray fluorescence|1=X-ray fluorescence}} analysis has revealed that the deceased were laid down without using wooden coffins. The front chamber appears to have originally been a passageway, but was modified at some unknown time. It has a flat ceiling, whereas the middle and rear chambers have a high, dome-shaped ceiling.
The 2.3 meter wide and 1.1 meter high waist stone on the back wall of the burial chamber and the wall on the right side of the middle chamber are decorated with continuous triangular patterns, using the three colors of red, black, and green on the {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} rock surface. The drawings also depict people, ships, shields, ''takatsuki'' (tall cups), {{ill|Bracken|en|Bracken|WD=Q258034|lt=Bracken|1=Bracken}} (''warabi'') patterns, concentric circles, and flowers. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included beads, pieces of iron arrowheads, pieces of {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}es, shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From Tosu Station on the JR Kagoshima Main Line, get off at Nishitetsu Bus "Tashiro Koen Iriguchi" and walk 5 minutes.

The {{nihongo|'''Tashiroōta Kofun'''|田代太田古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tashirohonmachi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tosu, Saga|en|Tosu, Saga|WD=Q860674|lt=Tosu|1=Tosu, Saga}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1926.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202916|title=田代太田古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Because the Kofun is located on the grounds of a private house, and there are preservation issues, it is closed to the public except for a special exhibition held once a year.
==Overview== The Tashiroōta Kofun is located on a hill between the Oki River and the Yamashita River, about 51 meters above sea level. The hill extends southeast from the 312.3 meter Shashigamine, which is a tributary peak of the Sefuri Mountains. It is a round {{nihono|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a dimater of 42-meters and a height of six meters, constructed in two tiers. However, due to urban encroachment, part of the tumulus was destroyed, so much remains unknown regarding its form and scale. In the surrounding area are many {{nihongo|''[[Zenpō-kōen-fun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tumuli.
A fragment of a cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been found on the tumulus. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry structure, consisting of a front chamber, a middle chamber, and a rear chamber, orientated to the south. It has a length of approximately nine meters, and contains corpse beds for three bodies in the rear chamber and two bodies in the middle chamber. {{ill|X-ray fluorescence|en|X-ray fluorescence|WD=Q898974|lt=X-ray fluorescence|1=X-ray fluorescence}} analysis has revealed that the deceased were laid down without using wooden coffins. The front chamber appears to have originally been a passageway, but was modified at some unknown time. It has a flat ceiling, whereas the middle and rear chambers have a high, dome-shaped ceiling.
The 2.3 meter wide and 1.1 meter high waist stone on the back wall of the burial chamber and the wall on the right side of the middle chamber are decorated with continuous triangular patterns, using the three colors of red, black, and green on the {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} rock surface. The drawings also depict people, ships, shields, ''takatsuki'' (tall cups), {{ill|Bracken|en|Bracken|WD=Q258034|lt=Bracken|1=Bracken}} (''warabi'') patterns, concentric circles, and flowers. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included beads, pieces of iron arrowheads, pieces of {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}es, shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. From these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From Tosu Station on the JR Kagoshima Main Line, get off at Nishitetsu Bus "Tashiro Koen Iriguchi" and walk 5 minutes.
